description: An instrument for writing or drawing with ink; the tool that makes thought visible. 'Phari' names what traces meaning—ink flowing into word.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes expression. 'Pha' /ɸa/ opens with creative breath, intention forming. Then 'ri' traces the line, marks appearing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo phari' (the pen). Works with 'shena' (paper) for writing. Related to 'sheli' (brush) but for finer, more precise marks.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Writing as practice—the pen slows thought, making intention visible and considered.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The pen preserves—knowledge, story, wisdom recorded for future minds.
